Fern Polrrrcer- PRACTTcEs C ovrMrssroN
.128 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento. CA 9581,1-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
March 4,2009
Delores Gaston
District Fair Business Assistant
Dixon May Fair
P.O. Box 459
Dixon, CA 95620
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-09-056
Dear Ms. Gaston:
This letter is in response to your request for written advice of March 4, 2009. Your
inquiry presents issues that are outside the purview of the Political Reform Act ("Act";1. The
Fair Political Practices Commission has no jurisdiction to interpret or advise on provisions
conceming state incompatible activities of state officers and employees as describe in
Govemment Code Section 19990. Thus, pursuant to Regulation 18329, copy enclosed, we are
declining to provide advice. You may wish to seek guidance of the Attorney General's Office.
Should you need assistance in the future, you may contact me at (916) 322-56G0.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Counsel
By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Division
Jwwjl
Enclosure
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statubry
ret'erences are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
CommissionarecontainedinSections l8ll0through l8997of Title2of theCalitbrniaCodeof Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2. Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. unless orherwise indicated.

sections.conclusion: The Fair Political Practices Commission has no jurisdiction to interpret or advise on provisions concerning state incompatible activities of state officers and employees as describe in Government Code Section 19990. Thus, pursuant to Regulation 18329, the FPPC is declining to provide advice.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked for FPPC advice regarding state incompatible activities of state officers and employees under Government Code Section 19990.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the inquiry concerns state incompatible activities of state officers and employees, which are outside the purview of the Political Reform Act and the FPPC's jurisdiction. The requester was directed to seek guidance from the Attorney General's Office.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with clear jurisdiction limitation. No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections present, but substantive legal question and FPPC's declination reason are explicitly stated in body text. Document references Regulation 18329 as basis for declination.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester asked for FPPC advice regarding state incompatible activities of state officers and employees under Government Code Section 19990.

CONCLUSION: The Fair Political Practices Commission has no jurisdiction to interpret or advise on provisions concerning state incompatible activities of state officers and employees as describe in Government Code Section 19990. Thus, pursuant to Regulation 18329, the FPPC is declining to provide advice.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide written advice on a request from Delores Gaston regarding state incompatible activities, stating the matter falls outside its jurisdictional authority under the Political Reform Act.
